title: NOC 41321 – Career Development Practitioners and Career Counsellors (Except Education) | GTR Immigration
description: Career Development Practitioners and Career Counsellors (Except Education) under NOC 41321 are professionals who assist individuals in finding employment, navigating career transitions, and overcoming barriers to workforce participation. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role supports Canada’s employment, rehabilitation, and workforce development systems. Unlike educational counsellors (NOC 41320), these professionals primarily work in employment services, community agencies, correctional facilities, and corporate HR environments.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group413 – Professional occupations in social and community servicesMinor Group4132 – Educational and employment counsellorsNOC Code41321

duties: Career Development Practitioners perform some or all of the following duties:
Interview clients to assess employment history and goals
Assist with résumé writing and interview preparation
Develop job readiness and job search strategies
Refer clients to rehabilitation, financial aid, or training programs
Provide career transition support for established workers
Collect and analyze labour market information
Advise employers on HR and employment-related issues
Provide consulting services to community organizations
Provide advocacy services related to employment legislation
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in:Employment counsellingCareer developmentHuman resources developmentPsychologyEducationSocial services
Master’s degree may be required in:Counselling psychologyEducational psychologyDevelopmental psychologySocial work
Vocational rehabilitation certification may be required
In Quebec:Membership in l'Ordre des conseillers et conseillères d'orientation du Québec required for protected titles
Membership in l'Ordre des conseillers et conseillères d'orientation du Québec required for protected titles
In New Brunswick:Membership in the College of Counselling Therapists required for “Licensed career counsellor”
Membership in the College of Counselling Therapists required for “Licensed career counsellor”
In Alberta, BC, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia, and Ontario:Certified Career Development Professional (CCDP) certification may be required
Certified Career Development Professional (CCDP) certification may be required
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment and certification recognition.
salary_prospects: Career Development Practitioners and Career Counsellors typically earn between:
$40,500 – $80,300 per year, depending on province, experience, and certification level.
Higher compensation may be available in Northern territories and senior consulting roles.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41321 is stable and growing due to:
Increased need for rehabilitation and reintegration services
Labour shortages and career transition support
Demand may fluctuate with economic cycles.
labour_market_demand: Career Development Practitioners and Career Counsellors (Except Education) are employed in:
Higher demand exists in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Northern territories.
Stable employment in government and community sectors
Growing demand in workforce transition programs
Opportunities to work with diverse populations
Pathways into HR and workforce policy roles
Professionals with experience in rehabilitation, Indigenous employment programs, and newcomer settlement services are particularly valued.
pathways: Certification may be required before full professional practice.
